name: Tagliatelle with Red Seafood Sauce - Country Living
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Fresh thyme leaves or 1/2 tsp Dried thyme leaves
1 lb Large shrimp, shelled but with tails left on, and deveined
1 lb Cod fillets, cut into 1-inch chunks
1 lb Cleaned calamari (squid), thawed if frozen (see Note)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat 2 Tbsp olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic, thyme, red pepper flakes, and 1/4 tsp salt; cook for 2 minutes. Add shrimp and cod to the skillet and sauté with a slotted spoon until shrimp and cod are almost cooked through, about 3 minutes. Cut calamari crosswise into 1/2-inch-thick rings; add to seafood mixture and cook for 1 minute (do not overcook as calamari will toughen). Remove seafood to a large bowl or plate; cover to keep warm and set aside.
2. Add the remaining 3 tbsp olive oil to the skillet. Stir in diced tomatoes with their juice, white wine, sugar, and the remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt to make the sauce; cover and simmer for 10 minutes. Meanwhile, cook tagliatelle following package directions. Uncover the sauce and cook for an additional 5 minutes. Remove from heat and gently stir in the reserved seafood.
3. If desired, just before serving, steam mussels until they open. Discard any that don't open. To serve, transfer cooked pasta to a serving platter, top with sauce and seafood mixture, and toss until well combined. Garnish platter with mussels and fresh thyme sprig, if desired. Note: Grippa Brand calamari, cleaned and frozen in 2 1/2-lb packages, can be mail ordered from BG Lobster & Shrimp Corp. Call (800) 221-7664.
